---
title: "LCSM-181: No-reverse-coupling comparator (LOO baseline for LCSM-081)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LCSM-181 is the pre-specified **LOO comparator** for LCSM-081: identical in
every respect — outcomes `TE` / `TR` / `W`, arm × window change intercepts,
the `hs` / `erbto` / `deapp_c` adjuster block, the `TR → TE` confounder
coupling, the same observed cells — except that the two reverse couplings
($g_{W \to TE}$, $g_{W \to TR}$) are removed, so prior word reading no longer
enters the taught-vocabulary change equations.

PSIS-LOO of LCSM-081 against this model is the design note's "does the reverse
edge earn its place predictively" readout
(`notes/202607141030-time-lagged-model-designs.md`): the reverse-coupling
hypothesis needs LCSM-081 to be **not LOO-worse** than this baseline, alongside
the couplings' own direction and credible intervals. This model makes no
substantive claim of its own — its coupling table (the `TR → TE` adjuster term
and self-feedbacks) is reported for completeness only.

{{< include _partials/_results_lcsm.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
